quoteFirst: At 3 p.m., therefore, on the 27th of August, the tide off Hastings would have been running eastward, and would have continued to do so until about 7.50 p.m. And on the 28th of August, which Airy wrongly assumes to have been the day of Caesar’s landing, the tide off Hastings would have turned eastward about
quoteLast: , and would have continued to run in that direction until about 8.53.
title: Ancient Britain and the Invasions of Julius Caesar
